blurb: The Alan Turing Institute is the UK's national institute for data science and AI, conducting research and informing policy across AI safety, defence, public services and frontier-AI readiness.

publications.title: New research will help UK prepare for next wave of frontier AI
publications.summary: Turing announces research aimed at helping the UK prepare for the next wave of frontier AI capabilities — directly policy-relevant to UK frontier-AI readiness.

publications.title: George Williamson joins the Turing as new CEO
publications.summary: Leadership change: George Williamson appointed as the Alan Turing Institute's new CEO.

publications.title: Turing chair Doug Gurr to step down following appointment to permanent CMA role
publications.summary: Turing chair Doug Gurr steps down after his appointment to a permanent role at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) — a notable AI-governance/regulator crossover.

publications.title: New project to build trust in AI for air traffic control
publications.summary: New Turing project focused on building trust in AI systems for air traffic control — applied AI-assurance work in a safety-critical domain.

publications.title: Building and procuring sustainable Defence AI will boost force resilience
publications.summary: Turing analysis arguing that sustainable procurement and building of Defence AI will improve force resilience — defence-AI policy signal.

notes: Harvested 2026-06-27 via OpenClaw headless browser. turing.ac.uk is hard-Cloudflare-gated (interactive turnstile): the /news landing page passes intermittently but individual article pages and the /news/publications policy-submissions index re-trigger the 'Just a moment...' managed challenge that headless cannot auto-solve. Titles + URLs are first-party verified from the /news listing; per-article publication DATES could not be read (articles blocked), so date=null rather than invented — same convention as the ICO undated items. The /news/publications index (RUSI-style policy submissions) remains gated; recommend a date backfill once a turnstile-capable fetch path exists. Selected the 5 most AI/policy-relevant of ~10 listed items; omitted non-policy items (Google Summer of Code, maritime gender diversity, Wooldridge honorary fellow, AI-for-Science showcase).
